completion_status_mapping.NotStarted: Not in capabilities.json. Reserved for capabilities the product will need but which have not yet been admitted to the graph.
completion_status_mapping.Planned: status: Accepted; delivered_by_milestone status: Planned; no plan yet, or plan stub is Draft. Sequenced for a milestone, plan not promoted to Ready / Awaiting Approval.
completion_status_mapping.Ready: status: Accepted; delivered_by_milestone status: Planned or Active; a Ready or Awaiting Approval plan exists, and the task that advances the capability is in tasks.json with status Ready. Dependencies are satisfied.
completion_status_mapping.InProgress: status: Accepted; a task with this capability in its delivered_by_tasks has status InProgress in tasks.json. Implementation has started on a branch.
completion_status_mapping.Delivered: status: Accepted; implementation merged on a branch with a commit; the implementation report exists; the milestones.json evidence array lists the commit. The receipts have not been linked in every consumer, or one of the consumer milestones is still open.
completion_status_mapping.Verified: status: Done; or status: Accepted with milestone status: Done AND every consumed_by dependency is also Verified. Implementation is in place, validation is green, and the evidence is linked.
completion_status_mapping.Blocked: status: Accepted; a Blocked task in tasks.json references the capability in its blocker field. The next task cannot start until the blocker is resolved.
completion_status_mapping.Deferred: status: Accepted; delivered_by_milestone status: Deferred in milestones.json. The roadmap defers the milestone; the work is in the backlog, not in the active queue.

capabilities.title: IProvider base contract
capabilities.description: The base metadata contract every family contract inherits from. Carries Id, DisplayName, DescribeAsync, HealthAsync, ConfigureAsync.
capabilities.product_outcome: Every family contract (IAgentRuntimeProvider, IGitProvider, etc.) shares a single base shape that the registry can enumerate, the health service can poll, and the composition root can configure.
acceptance_criteria: IProvider carries Id, DisplayName, DescribeAsync, HealthAsync, ConfigureAsync.
Every family contract (C-002..C-009) inherits from IProvider.
The IProviderRegistry can enumerate, resolve by ProviderId, and apply Enabled/Healthy filter (C-010).

capabilities.title: IAgentRuntimeProvider family
capabilities.description: Providers that execute agent models (Ollama Launch, Ollama API, Claude, OpenAI, Codex, custom command runtimes).
capabilities.product_outcome: The platform can launch a coding agent through a registered runtime provider; users pick a runtime and a model from the registry, not from a hard-coded list.
acceptance_criteria: IAgentRuntimeProvider extends IProvider; LaunchAsync returns an IAsyncEnumerable<string> stream.
OllamaLaunchProvider smoke test (M4-D) and a vertical slice (M6) are green.
Only_CompositionRoot_MayReference_ConcreteProviders architecture test is active and green.

capabilities.title: IGitProvider family
capabilities.description: Providers that read and operate on source control (Git).
capabilities.product_outcome: The platform can read repository state, list branches, and compute diffs through a registered IGitProvider, so the worktree, review, and disposition surfaces can render Git data without bypassing the registry.
acceptance_criteria: IGitProvider extends IProvider; StatusAsync / DiffAsync / LogAsync return a typed result.
GitProvider (M4-D) smoke test is green; composition-root architecture test is active.
IWorktreeProvider (C-005) consumes IGitProvider through the family-scoped registry.

capabilities.title: ITerminalProvider family
capabilities.description: Providers that run shell commands (PowerShell, Windows Terminal, WSL, Git Bash).
capabilities.product_outcome: The platform can open a terminal in the developer's preferred shell through a registered ITerminalProvider, so the user can drop into a worktree from the application without leaving it.
acceptance_criteria: ITerminalProvider extends IProvider; OpenAsync returns a typed session descriptor.
NativeTerminalProvider smoke test (M6) is green; composition-root architecture test is active.

capabilities.title: IWorktreeProvider family
capabilities.description: Providers that create and manage isolated Git worktrees (native Git worktree, Treehouse).
capabilities.product_outcome: The platform can create, list, and dispose isolated Git worktrees through a registered IWorktreeProvider, so every run gets its own working tree without contaminating the developer's main checkout.
acceptance_criteria: IWorktreeProvider extends IProvider; CreateAsync / ListAsync / DisposeAsync return a typed Worktree handle.
NativeWorktreeProvider (M5) smoke test is green; consumes IGitProvider through the family-scoped registry.
Treehouse is integrated as a second provider (M5 dogfood).

capabilities.title: IQualityGateProvider family
capabilities.description: Providers that run a quality gate against a worktree (No Mistakes, built-in pass-through or disabled provider).
capabilities.product_outcome: The platform can run a quality gate against an isolated worktree through a registered IQualityGateProvider; the gate's pass / fail is rendered in the run surface and is part of the worktree's disposition.
acceptance_criteria: IQualityGateProvider extends IProvider; RunAsync returns a typed QualityGateResult.
NativeQualityGateProvider (M7) and No Mistakes (M7 dogfood) are wired through the composition root.
No_Secrets_In_Configuration and No_Secrets_In_Logs architecture tests are active and green.

capabilities.title: IReviewProvider family
capabilities.description: Providers that review an artefact (native HTML review, Lavish Axi).
capabilities.product_outcome: The platform can route an artefact (a design-system page, a generated report) to a registered IReviewProvider; the review's findings are rendered and persisted as part of the artefact's history.
acceptance_criteria: IReviewProvider extends IProvider; ReviewAsync returns a typed ReviewReport.
NativeReviewProvider (M7) is the baseline; Lavish Axi is wired as a second provider once installed.
No_Secrets_In_Configuration and No_Secrets_In_Logs architecture tests are active and green.

capabilities.title: IAutonomousLoopProvider family
capabilities.description: Providers that perform bounded autonomous iteration (GNHF).
capabilities.product_outcome: The platform can drive a bounded series of agent launches and quality gates under a registered IAutonomousLoopProvider; the loop is observable, cancellable, and recoverable.
acceptance_criteria: IAutonomousLoopProvider extends IProvider; RunAsync returns an IAsyncEnumerable<LoopEvent>.
NativeAutonomousLoopProvider (M8) and GNHF (M8 dogfood) are wired through the composition root.
NativeProviders_Use_ApplicationServices_Not_Implementation architecture test is active and green.

capabilities.title: IOrchestrationProvider family
capabilities.description: Providers that perform multi-agent orchestration (native orchestration, Firstmate through WSL).
capabilities.product_outcome: The platform can coordinate a graph of agents, worktrees, and gates under a registered IOrchestrationProvider; the orchestration is observable, recoverable, and bounded.
acceptance_criteria: IOrchestrationProvider extends IProvider; CoordinateAsync returns an IAsyncEnumerable<OrchestrationEvent>.
NativeOrchestrationProvider (M8) and Firstmate through WSL (M8 dogfood) are wired through the composition root.
NativeProviders_Use_ApplicationServices_Not_Implementation architecture test is active and green.

capabilities.title: IProviderRegistry and family-scoped registries
capabilities.description: The cross-family registry and the eight family-scoped registries. Enumerate registered providers, resolve by ProviderId, apply Enabled/Healthy filter.
capabilities.product_outcome: Every family has a typed registry; pages and services resolve providers by ProviderId, never by importing a concrete implementation directly. The cross-family registry surfaces the health state to the dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: IProviderRegistry and the eight family-scoped registries exist with Enumerate / Resolve / SetEnabled.
Pages_Resolve_Providers_Through_Registry and Pages_DoNotReference_ConcreteProviders architecture tests are active and green.
Fake providers for every family are wired in the composition root (M4-C).

capabilities.title: Composition root
capabilities.description: The set of files in AiEng.Platform.App/Composition/ that wire concrete provider implementations into DI. The only place in the solution that may reference Providers.<X> projects directly.
capabilities.product_outcome: Every concrete provider implementation is wired into DI in a single, audited location; Pages, Components, Application, and Domain never reference a concrete provider directly.
acceptance_criteria: AiEng.Platform.App/Composition/ exists; only this folder references Providers.<X> projects.
ServiceCollectionExtensions.AddProviderComposition() is the single composition extension; called once in Program.cs.
Only_CompositionRoot_MayReference_ConcreteProviders architecture test is active and green.

capabilities.title: IProcessRunner
capabilities.description: The only legal caller of Process.Start in the platform. Exposes RunAsync (streaming) and RunToCompletionAsync (one-shot).
capabilities.product_outcome: Every process the platform spawns goes through IProcessRunner; no module outside AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure calls Process.Start directly.

commits: feat(m4-a.1): add infrastructure project skeleton with IProcessRunner, ICredentialVault, IPlatformInfo, and on-disk IProjectStore
feat(m4-a.2): enable AppProjectCard.Open action via IProcessRunner

acceptance_criteria: AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure is created (M4-A); IProcessRunner has RunAsync and RunToCompletionAsync.
No_DirectProcessStart_OutsideInfrastructure architecture test is active and green.
At least one IProcessRunner call site exists in the App layer (M4-A.2: the Open action on AppProjectCard).
completed_criteria: AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure is created (M4-A); IProcessRunner has RunAsync and RunToCompletionAsync.
At least one IProcessRunner call site exists in the App layer (M4-A.2: the Open action on AppProjectCard).

capabilities.title: ICredentialVault
capabilities.description: The contract for reading and writing secrets. Real backing store is Windows Credential Manager.
capabilities.product_outcome: Every secret the platform reads or writes goes through ICredentialVault; the application never embeds a credential in configuration, code, or a log line.
acceptance_criteria: AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure is created (M4-A); ICredentialVault is implemented on Windows Credential Manager.
No_Secrets_In_Configuration and No_Secrets_In_Logs architecture tests are active and green.

capabilities.title: IClock
capabilities.description: The time abstraction. Exposes UtcNow. Used by every service that produces a timestamp.
capabilities.product_outcome: Every service that produces a timestamp reads it from IClock; tests can freeze time deterministically by substituting a fake clock.
acceptance_criteria: AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure is created (M4-A); IClock is implemented and injected into every service that produces a timestamp.

capabilities.title: IHostCapabilitiesService
capabilities.description: The host's capability detection. Detects git, ollama, powershell.exe, wsl.exe, wt.exe, bash.exe. Produces a typed HostCapabilities report at host startup.
capabilities.product_outcome: At host startup the platform reports which external tools are installed and reachable; the dashboard renders the report and the registry disables providers whose binaries are absent.
capabilities.next_task: T-025 (M4-B.2 first session — AppCapabilityList + AppKeyValueList design-system components; Ready)

commits: feat(m4-b.1): add IHostCapabilitiesService contract and SystemHostCapabilitiesService implementation

acceptance_criteria: IHostCapabilitiesService detects git, ollama, powershell.exe, wsl.exe, wt.exe, bash.exe at host startup.
Capabilities_Resolved_Through_Service architecture test is active and green.
completed_criteria: M4-B.1 (T-024): IHostCapabilitiesService contract + SystemHostCapabilitiesService implementation + AddHostCapabilities composition root + 20 unit tests are delivered. 343 passed, 0 failed, 9 skipped (per ADR-016 / M4-D). Pre-M4-B.1 baseline was 323 passed; +20 new M4-B.1 unit tests.
architecture_tests: Capabilities_Resolved_Through_Service (deferred to M4-B.3 per the M4-B.1 plan section 14.1 Deviations)

capabilities.title: IProjectService and IProjectStore
capabilities.description: The application service for projects and the persistence contract. M3 in-memory; M4-A durable.
capabilities.product_outcome: A developer can register a Git repository (a folder on disk) through the project service; the platform owns the Project entity end-to-end (register, list, rename, delete).
acceptance_criteria: IProjectService and IProjectStore exist; in-memory store lands in M3, durable on-disk store lands in M4-A.
Providers_Resolve_Project_Through_Service architecture test is active and green.

capabilities.title: IHistoryStore
capabilities.description: The persistence contract for runs, reviews, gates, and dispositions. Append-only event log.
capabilities.product_outcome: Every run, every review, every gate, and every disposition is persisted to IHistoryStore; the history survives an application restart and is queryable from the dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: IHistoryStore is an append-only event log; events cover run, review, gate, and disposition.
History_Routed_Through_Store architecture test is active and green.

capabilities.title: IProviderHealthService
capabilities.description: The periodic health-check service. Polls every registered provider and reports ProviderHealth (Healthy, Degraded, Unhealthy) through the registries.
capabilities.product_outcome: Every registered provider is polled periodically; the dashboard renders the health state; the registries hide Unhealthy providers from selection.
acceptance_criteria: IProviderHealthService polls every registered provider on a configurable interval; reports ProviderHealth.
The cross-family registry filters Unhealthy providers from selection by default.

capabilities.title: INavigationService
capabilities.description: The route-aware navigation service. The sidebar and the breadcrumb are data-driven from a route registry, not hard-coded.
capabilities.product_outcome: Every page is reachable through the navigation registry; the sidebar and the breadcrumb are data-driven from a single source of truth; adding a page requires only a RouteMetadata attribute.
acceptance_criteria: INavigationRegistry, RouteMetadata, RouteRegistry exist; the AppSidebar is data-driven from the registry.
Every page declares its route metadata through RouteMetadataAttribute.
Pages_AreReachable_Through_Registry architecture test is active and green.
AppTopBar exists with Leading and Trailing slots; the trailing slot hosts AppThemeToggle and AppUserAvatarSlot (M2.3).
AppBreadcrumb walks the M2.2 registry's Parent chain (M2.3).
AppBreadcrumb is wired into AppPageHeader.Breadcrumbs on pages that use AppPageHeader (M2.3).
completed_criteria: AppLayout, AppEmptyLayout, AppShellRegion exist (M2.1 — the shell foundation the navigation registry composes against).
INavigationRegistry, RouteMetadata, RouteRegistry, RouteMetadataAttribute exist; AppSidebar, AppSidebarItem, AppNavItem are data-driven from the registry (M2.2).
Every page in Components/Pages/ declares its route metadata through RouteMetadataAttribute (M2.2).
Pages_AreReachable_Through_Registry architecture test is active and green (M2.2).
AppTopBar exists with Leading and Trailing slots; the trailing slot hosts AppThemeToggle and AppUserAvatarSlot (M2.3 — replaces the M2.1 AppTopBarSlot placeholder).
AppThemeToggle relocated to the top bar; the theme state persists via localStorage and applies via the data-theme attribute (M2.3).
AppBreadcrumb walks the M2.2 registry's Parent chain; aria-current=page is set on the current item; separators are aria-hidden (M2.3).
AppBreadcrumb is wired into AppPageHeader.Breadcrumbs on DesignSystem.razor (M2.3).

capabilities.title: Design system catalogue
capabilities.description: The catalogue of reusable Blazor components documented in docs/design-system.md. Every reusable component has a public surface, a class slot assignment, and a versioning rule.
capabilities.product_outcome: Every reusable Blazor component is documented in docs/design-system.md with a public surface, a class slot, and a versioning rule; the /design-system page renders the catalogue from the docs.
acceptance_criteria: docs/design-system.md is the catalogue; every reusable component has a public surface, a class slot, and a versioning rule.
Pages_Use_DesignSystem_Components_Not_DOM and App_DoesNotReference_Providers_Implementations architecture tests are active and green.
The /design-system page renders the catalogue.
completed_criteria: docs/design-system.md is the catalogue; every reusable component has a public surface, a class slot, and a versioning rule.
Pages_Use_DesignSystem_Components_Not_DOM and App_DoesNotReference_Providers_Implementations architecture tests are active and green.
The /design-system page renders the catalogue.

capabilities.title: IStreamingChannel
capabilities.description: The streaming channel for long-running work. Pages subscribe to IStreamingChannel; the channel subscribes to a provider's IAsyncEnumerable.
capabilities.product_outcome: Pages render a long-running run's stdout / stderr in real time through IStreamingChannel; the channel abstracts the provider's IAsyncEnumerable so a page does not depend on a particular runtime.
acceptance_criteria: IStreamingChannel exposes SubscribeAsync; the channel wraps a provider's IAsyncEnumerable without leaking it to the page.
The run-observation page renders the stream in real time without blocking the Blazor render thread.

capabilities.title: IProjectIntelligenceReader
capabilities.description: The read-side application service that exposes a typed ProjectIntelligenceSnapshot built from the M0.5 structured state (.ai/state/*.json). The dashboard consumes only this abstraction; no page parses JSON or Markdown directly.
capabilities.product_outcome: The /dashboard page renders the project intelligence state (current milestone, current task, current slice, branch, last commit, build status, test status, product progress, capability progress, implemented capabilities, plans awaiting approval, next recommended action) by consuming IProjectIntelligenceReader through DI. M3+ slices add new data sources but do not modify the reader.
acceptance_criteria: IProjectIntelligenceReader.GetSnapshotAsync returns a typed ProjectIntelligenceSnapshot.
ProjectIntelligenceReader reads .ai/state/*.json and produces the snapshot deterministically.
The /dashboard page consumes IProjectIntelligenceReader through DI; no page parses JSON or Markdown.
Pages_Resolve_State_Through_Reader architecture test is active and green.
The dashboard renders the M0.5-data sections in the Populated state (per ADR-014) and the M3+-data sections in the Empty state.
completed_criteria: IProjectIntelligenceReader.GetSnapshotAsync returns a typed ProjectIntelligenceSnapshot.
ProjectIntelligenceReader reads .ai/state/*.json and produces the snapshot deterministically.
The /dashboard page consumes IProjectIntelligenceReader through DI; no page parses JSON or Markdown.
Pages_Resolve_State_Through_Reader architecture test is active and green.
The dashboard renders the M0.5-data sections in the Populated state (per ADR-014) and the M3+-data sections in the Empty state.

capabilities.title: AppCapabilityList
capabilities.description: The data-owning four-state design-system component that renders an IReadOnlyList<HostCapability> as a list of AppCard entries. Each card shows the Key, an AppStatusDot (Success for Available=true, Error for Available=false), the Version (font-mono, muted), and an AppBadge 'Credential set' when CredentialAvailable is true. Supports the four child-content slots (Loading, Empty, Error, Populated) per docs/design-system.md section 5.4.
capabilities.product_outcome: The /diagnostics page (M4-B.3) renders the host capability report through AppCapabilityList; the user can see at a glance which host tools are installed, their versions, and which credentials are available. The component is also consumed by M5+ worktree and agent launch pages to surface provider dependencies.

commits: feat(m4-b.2): add AppCapabilityList + AppKeyValueList data-owning design-system components

acceptance_criteria: AppCapabilityList renders an IReadOnlyList<HostCapability> as a list of AppCard entries.
AppCapabilityList supports the four child-content slots (Loading, Empty, Error, Populated) per docs/design-system.md section 5.4.
AppCapabilityList renders an AppStatusDot Success for Available=true and Error for Available=false.
AppCapabilityList renders an AppBadge 'Credential set' when CredentialAvailable is true.
The populated list has aria-live=polite for screen reader announcements.
The /diagnostics page (M4-B.3) consumes AppCapabilityList through DI of IHostCapabilitiesService.
completed_criteria: AppCapabilityList renders an IReadOnlyList<HostCapability> as a list of AppCard entries.
AppCapabilityList supports the four child-content slots (Loading, Empty, Error, Populated) per docs/design-system.md section 5.4.
AppCapabilityList renders an AppStatusDot Success for Available=true and Error for Available=false.
AppCapabilityList renders an AppBadge 'Credential set' when CredentialAvailable is true.
The populated list has aria-live=polite for screen reader announcements.
M4-B.2 (T-025): 13 bUnit tests for AppCapabilityList are delivered and green; 13 bUnit tests for AppKeyValueList are delivered and green. The new files are CRLF and the format is canonical.

capabilities.title: AppKeyValueList
capabilities.description: The data-owning four-state design-system component that renders an IReadOnlyList<KeyValuePair<string, string>> as a definition list (dl) of key-value rows. Supports an AppKeyValueListFormat enum (Plain, Boolean, Code) that controls value rendering: Plain renders literal text, Boolean renders a check/cross icon for true/false, Code renders in a monospaced font. Supports the four child-content slots (Loading, Empty, Error, Populated) per docs/design-system.md section 5.4.
capabilities.product_outcome: The /diagnostics page (M4-B.3) renders host metadata (hostname, OS, architecture, time provider, etc.) through AppKeyValueList. The component is also consumed by M5+ pages that need to surface key-value configuration data (e.g., worktree settings, agent launch parameters).

commits: feat(m4-b.2): add AppCapabilityList + AppKeyValueList data-owning design-system components

acceptance_criteria: AppKeyValueList renders an IReadOnlyList<KeyValuePair<string, string>> as a definition list (dl).
AppKeyValueList supports an AppKeyValueListFormat enum (Plain, Boolean, Code).
AppKeyValueList supports the four child-content slots (Loading, Empty, Error, Populated) per docs/design-system.md section 5.4.
The populated list has aria-live=polite for screen reader announcements.
completed_criteria: AppKeyValueList renders an IReadOnlyList<KeyValuePair<string, string>> as a definition list (dl).
AppKeyValueList supports an AppKeyValueListFormat enum (Plain, Boolean, Code).
AppKeyValueList supports the four child-content slots (Loading, Empty, Error, Populated) per docs/design-system.md section 5.4.
M4-B.2 (T-025): 15 bUnit tests for AppKeyValueList are delivered and green; the AppKeyValueListFormat enum is added to Enums.cs. The new files are CRLF and the format is canonical.
